TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version 11.5.2600.32.W updated on MAX-DC-VDI-002 , MAX-DC-VDI-GI & AKT-DC-FS-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Reviewed the RMM portal and identified that the  MAX-DC-VDI-002 , MAX-DC-VDI-GI & AKT-DC-FS-01 were running an outdated version of ManageEngine.  downloaded and installed the latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) of the ManageEngine agent. scan the system with the  portal that the device now runs the updated agent version

TimeEntry.Title: Upgrade VMWare tools - AKT-DC-FS-01, MAX-DC-AD-01, MAX-DC-VDI-002.
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login to the vCenter and check the following machines needs to upgrade the VMware tool on it. Go to the VM and the initiate upgrade VMware tool on the machine successfully.

AKT-DC-FS-01, MAX-DC-AD-01, MAX-DC-VDI-002.

TimeEntry.Title: MyRMM Manage-Engine Latest version (11.5.2600.32.W) updated:- MAX-DC-VDI-001 and MAX-DC-AD-01
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the portal, Found New Agent Version (11.5.2600.32.W) was released, and Pushed the sensor from the portal but it failed so connected to the below machine ( MAX-DC-VDI-001 and MAX-DC-AD-01) from Manage Engine, Tried to update the version but got an error (old version is already installed on it) so removed the earlier installed version. Downloaded and installed the new version after verifying the same from the portal, Devices are up to date with the latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Cisco Umbrella Agent Syncing Issue with Portal for machines AKT-DC-FS-01,MAX-DC-AD-01,MAX-DC-VDI-001,MAX-DC-VDI-GI
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the Cisco Umbrella portal and identified that machines AKT-DC-FS-01,MAX-DC-AD-01,MAX-DC-VDI-001,MAX-DC-VDI-GI were experiencing syncing issues with the Cisco Umbrella portal. I then connected to the affected machines and addressed the network connectivity settings. After ensuring proper communication between the agent and the portal, the agent is now successfully syncing and communicating with the Cisco Umbrella cloud, with the correct status and configuration reflected in the portal.

TimeEntry.Title: Network Detective Scan MAX-April-2026
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into the rapid-fire tool and then initiate a network scan for MAX client. After Scan is complete for all then start generating the reports. then Go to the web and then generate the reports for MAX and copy the reports to Teams folder under Monthly scan folder

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version 0.14.162, replaced with Version 0.14.150 on MAX-DC-AD-01, AKT-DC-FS-01, MAX-DC-VDI-001, MAX-DC-VDI-002
TimeEntry.Notes: Huntress Agent updated with Version 0.14.162, replaced with Version 0.14.150 on MAX-DC-AD-01, AKT-DC-FS-01, MAX-DC-VDI-001, MAX-DC-VDI-002

TimeEntry.Title: Manage Engine Synchronization Issue for MAX Machine AKT-DC-FS-01 and MAX-DC-VDI-002
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into Manage engine portal and found the agent on machines AKT-DC-FS-01 and MAX-DC-VDI-002 not communication with portal. So connected to the machine, tried to restart the service, but nothing happened. So, Uninstall the Corrupt manage engine then download and the latest manage engine agent. After that agent on machine is communicating with the portal and working fine hence closing this ticket

TimeEntry.Title: Remote log in not working
TimeEntry.Notes: As per Wes's request, the VDI was not accessible from Omnissa, so I checked and found that the VDI was not showing in vCenter. After discussing the issue with the lead, we decided to restore the VDI from backup. The restoration was successful, and the VDI is now online and reachable. A confirmation email has been sent to Wes, and once we receive his confirmation, we will proceed to close the ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: MAX_DC_VDI_GI Probe will shutdown shortly.
TimeEntry.Notes: as per alert checked and found that VM was showing down and so logged into the V-center and powered on the VM and verified the services and verified on the portal as well the probe is showing up now.

TimeEntry.Title: Snapshot consolidation required - MAX-DC-AD-01.
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged in to vCenter and found the server MAX-DC-AD-01 is on Disk consolidation. Go the snapshots and start consolidation for the server and complete the process successfully and then login to the server and verify all the services are up and working fine.
